perturbation.original_text: A "Representative" of a Party includes any officer, director, shareholder, employee, advisor, attorney, consultant or agent of such Party.
perturbation.changed_text: A "Representative" of a Party only includes officers, directors, and employees of such Party.
perturbation.explanation: This change restricts the definition of 'Representative' to only include officers, directors, and employees, excluding advisors, attorneys, consultants, and agents. This contradicts sections that rely on the broader definition of 'Representative', such as Section 3 which mandates informing 'each such Representative' of the terms of the agreement. With the restricted definition, it is unclear whether the responsibilities outlined in Section 3 apply to external advisors or consultants, creating an ambiguity on which parties are bound by the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: REPRESENTATIVES. The Recipient will (a) provide the other Party with a list of its Representatives who are given access to any Evaluation Material, (b) inform each such Representative of the terms of this Agreement, and (c) obtain the agreement of each such Representative to abide by the terms of this Agreement. Each Party will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Representative' broadly, including advisors, attorneys, consultants, and agents. The changed text restricts this definition to only officers, directors, and employees. The contradicted text requires informing 'each such Representative' of the agreement terms and holding each party responsible for breaches by its Representatives. The new, narrower definition of 'Representative' creates a direct contradiction because it's now unclear if the agreement applies to advisors, attorneys, consultants, and agents, thus creating ambiguity in Section 3's responsibilities regarding who is bound by the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will maintain the strict confidentiality of any Evaluation Material provided to it or any of its Representatives by the other Party or any of the other Party's Representatives and will not disclose any part of it to any other person; provided, however, that it may disclose any such Evaluation Material or portions thereof to those of its Representatives who need to know such information in order to assist the Recipient in evaluating the advisability of entering into the Possible Transaction and it may disclose any such Evaluation Material in accordance with Paragraph 5 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient will maintain the strict confidentiality of any Evaluation Material provided to it or any of its Representatives by the other Party or any of the other Party's Representatives and will not disclose any part of it to any other person; provided, however, that it may disclose any such Evaluation Material or portions thereof to those of its Representatives who need to know such information in order to assist the Recipient in evaluating the advisability of entering into the Possible Transaction, and it may disclose any such Evaluation Material in accordance with Paragraph 5 hereof. Furthermore, the recipient is permitted to discuss the evaluation material with any potential financing sources as needed to secure funding.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a clause permitting the Recipient to disclose Evaluation Material to potential financing sources. This directly contradicts the initial statement of strict confidentiality and broadens the exceptions to an undefined group. The clause creates uncertainty as to the limits of permissible disclosure. The contradiction is with the primary purpose of the agreement which is the strict confidentiality of evaluation material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: USE OF EVALUATION MATERIAL. Any Evaluation Material provided to the Recipient hereunder will be used by the Recipient and its Representatives solely for the purpose of evaluating the Possible Transaction and will not be used by the Recipient in any way detrimental to the Provider or its stockholders. The Recipient will maintain the strict confidentiality of any Evaluation Material provided to it or any of its Representatives by the other Party or any of the other Party's Representatives and will not disclose any part of it to any other person; provided, however, that it may disclose any such Evaluation Material or portions thereof to those of its Representatives who need to know such information in order to assist the Recipient in evaluating the advisability of entering into the Possible Transaction and it may disclose any such Evaluation Material in accordance with Paragraph 5 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the recipient will maintain strict confidentiality and not disclose any part of the evaluation material to any other person, with limited exceptions for its representatives and Paragraph 5. The changed text adds 'Furthermore, the recipient is permitted to discuss the evaluation material with any potential financing sources as needed to secure funding.' This introduces a new exception, allowing disclosure to 'potential financing sources,' which directly contradicts the initial statement of strict confidentiality in both the original text and the contradicted text. This creates a clear in-text contradiction because it expands the permissible disclosures to a group beyond those initially defined, thereby creating uncertainty and conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives, only if the party had knowledge of the impending breach.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a condition that the Party is only responsible for breaches by its Representatives if it had knowledge of the impending breach. This creates an ambiguity as to the level of due diligence required by each Party to prevent breaches by its Representatives. This creates an internal contradiction as the original agreement created a strict liability standard for breaches by representatives. Section 4, which defines indemnification, now is contradicted by this change as it is no longer clear when a party must indemnify another for a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: INDEMNIFICATION. Each Party will indemnify, defend and hold harmless the other Party from and against any and all damages, losses, liabilities and costs that any other Party may sustain or incur as a result of any breach of this Agreement by such Party or any of its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that each party is responsible for any breach by its representatives. The changed text adds a condition that the party is only responsible if it had knowledge of the impending breach. This creates a contradiction with the indemnification clause, which states that each party will indemnify the other for any breach by its representatives. Under the original agreement, indemnification was required for any breach, but now it is only required if the party had knowledge of the impending breach. This is a direct contradiction as the scope of the indemnification is now conditional where it was previously not.

perturbation.original_text: In such event, the Recipient promptly will return to the Provider all copies of any Evaluation Material in its possession or in the possession of any of its Representatives furnished by such Provider or any of its Representatives, and the Recipient will destroy all analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by it or any of its Representatives or for their use containing or reflecting any Evaluation Material, whether in digital, written or other form.
perturbation.changed_text: In such event, the Recipient may, at its discretion, return to the Provider all copies of any Evaluation Material in its possession or in the possession of any of its Representatives furnished by such Provider or any of its Representatives, and the Recipient will destroy all analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by it or any of its Representatives or for their use containing or reflecting any Evaluation Material, whether in digital, written or other form.
perturbation.explanation: This change replaces 'will promptly return' with 'may, at its discretion, return', making the return of Evaluation Material optional. This directly contradicts the original obligation to return the material, introducing uncertainty about the recipient's duty after termination. The phrase 'destroy all analyses' remains mandatory, creating a contradiction: why destroy analyses based on material if the original material might not even be returned? This undermines the provider's ability to control its confidential information after the deal falls through. This contradicts the overall intention of the agreement to protect evaluation material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERMINATION. If any Party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it will promptly advise the other Parties of that decision. In such event, the Recipient promptly will return to the Provider all copies of any Evaluation Material in its possession or in the possession of any of its Representatives furnished by such Provider or any of its Representatives, and the Recipient will destroy all analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by it or any of its Representatives or for their use containing or reflecting any Evaluation Material, whether in digital, written or other form. Any destruction required pursuant to this Paragraph 6 will be certified in writing to the Provider by an authorized officer supervising such destruction.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text both state that the Recipient 'will promptly return' the Evaluation Material. The changed text modifies this to 'may, at its discretion, return,' creating a direct contradiction. The original and contradicted sections mandate return, while the changed section makes it optional, thus breaking a previously firm obligation. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' by creating an inconsistency in the obligations.

perturbation.original_text: All Parties' obligations under this Agreement will expire one year from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: All Parties' obligations under this Agreement, with the exception of the non-disclosure obligations in Section 7, will expire one year from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an exception to the one-year expiration for the non-disclosure obligations in Section 7, implying that those obligations survive beyond the one-year term. However, the non-disclosure obligations are broad and intertwined with other obligations that expire. This creates an ambiguity because Section 7 prevents a party from disclosing that discussions are even taking place. If the agreement expires, but Section 7 remains in effect, it's unclear whether a party can acknowledge that the agreement existed after one year, creating a conflict with the intent of limiting the agreement's overall lifespan.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-DISCLOSURE. Without the prior written consent of the other Party, each Party will not, and will direct its Representatives not to, disclose to any person that any investigations, discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a Possible Transaction, that such Party has requested or received Evaluation Material from the other Party, or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any Possible Transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates an in-text contradiction. The original text stated that all obligations expire after one year. The changed text adds an exception for non-disclosure obligations in Section 7, implying that those obligations survive beyond the one-year term. This contradicts the intent of limiting the agreement's overall lifespan and creates an ambiguity. The contradicted text is the NON-DISCLOSURE section, which states that neither party can disclose that any investigations, discussions or negotiations are taking place, which directly conflicts with the main agreement ending in a year but the NDA remaining. This is a strong contradiction because it's unclear whether a party can acknowledge that the agreement existed after one year, which is a key aspect of ending the agreement.
